meta.title: Fourth of July — New Hampshire
meta.description: Fourth of July week at Mom & Dad's lake house in Melvin Village, NH. Kristina and family coming from Nashville for the same dates.

bookings.label: American Airlines · STL → BOS
bookings.detail: Jul 1 · AA 3814 (Envoy Air) · STL 9:39 AM → BOS 1:24 PM. Ticketed through BUF — deplane at BOS, do not board BOS→BUF leg. No checked bags.
bookings.notes: Skiplagged fare: booked STL→BUF via BOS. Deplane at BOS. Carry-on only — no checked bags or they go to BUF.

bookings.label: Southwest · BOS → STL
bookings.detail: Jul 7 · WN 754 · BOS 6:25 PM → STL 8:30 PM. Seats 12A/B/C/D (Preferred). Basic fare.
bookings.notes: Meghan 12A, Michael 12B, Louise 12C, Kenna 12D. Basic fare — carryon + personal item only.

bookings.label: Enterprise · Midsize (Corolla) at BOS
bookings.detail: Jul 1 1:00 PM → Jul 7 6:00 PM. Midsize Car (Toyota Corolla or Similar). 15 Transportation Way, East Boston.
bookings.notes: Booked via Capital One Travel. Platinum status with Enterprise. Free cancellation before pick-up.

events.text: ✈ AA 3814 · STL → BOS · Deplane at BOS (skiplagged!)
Arrive BOS. Deplane immediately — do NOT board BOS→BUF leg
Pick up rental car — Enterprise, 15 Transportation Way
🚗 Drive BOS → Melvin Village (~2 hrs via I-93 N → Rte 109)
Arrive at Mom & Dad's! Settle in, unpack, lake time

items.text: CARRY-ON ONLY — NO CHECKED BAGS. This is a skiplagged fare (STL→BUF via BOS). Checked bags will be sent to Buffalo. Pack everything in carry-ons + personal items.
Pack for a week at the lake: swimsuits, sunscreen, bug spray, lake toys for Louise
Bring car seats for Louise (and Kenna's booster if needed). Enterprise won't have them
Download offline entertainment for Louise — 2.5 hr flight + 2 hr drive
Charge all devices. Pack chargers in carry-on
This ticket is booked as STL → BUF with a connection at BOS. You are deplaning at BOS and skipping the BOS→BUF leg. This is legal but against airline policy
NO CHECKED BAGS. Any checked luggage will be routed to Buffalo, not Boston
Do NOT tell gate agents or crew you're getting off at BOS. Just deplane normally when the door opens
Do NOT use AAdvantage frequent flyer numbers on this booking — skiplagging can trigger account flags
If the flight is cancelled or rerouted, AA will rebook you to BUF (your ticketed destination), not BOS. That's the risk
Savings: ~$81 for 4 passengers vs. booking STL→BOS directly ($634 vs $553)
Enterprise Rent-A-Car · 15 Transportation Way, East Boston, MA 02128
Midsize Car (Toyota Corolla or similar). Confirmation: H-1596322716COUNT
Paid with Capital One Venture X miles (60,686 miles). Booked via Capital One Travel
Enterprise Platinum status — skip the counter, go straight to the aisle. You may get a free upgrade
Drive: I-93 N → I-95 N → Rte 16 N → Rte 109 to Melvin Village. ~2 hours depending on traffic
If you need a stop: The Common Man Roadside in Epsom, NH (1890 Dover Rd) is a great halfway point — you've stopped there before
Stop for groceries on the way up — Hannaford in Alton or Wolfeboro is the closest full grocery store to Melvin Village
Gas up before leaving the highway — stations are sparse near the lake
Louise will likely be wiped from travel. Plan for an easy first evening — lake, dinner at the house, early bedtime

timeline.title: First Full Day at the Lake

events.text: Sleep in — decompress from travel day
Morning: Dock time, swimming, lake toys with Louise
Afternoon: Explore Wolfeboro (~15 min drive)
Dinner at the house with Mom & Dad

items.text: No fixed plans — ease into lake life. Let Louise and Kenna get comfortable with the house and water
Check if Kristina's family has arrived or when they're expected
Sunscreen + bug spray protocol — NH lake country means mosquitoes at dusk

details.title: Things to Do — Lake Winnipesaukee Area

items.text: Wolfeboro: Self-proclaimed 'oldest summer resort in America.' Great downtown with shops, restaurants, ice cream on the waterfront. ~15 min drive
Brewster Beach (Wolfeboro): Public beach on the lake, calmer water, good for Louise. Free parking for town visitors
Wright Museum: WWII museum in Wolfeboro. More for the adults/Kenna if there's a rainy stretch
Ice cream: Bailey's Bubble in Wolfeboro or the Yum Yum Shop in Center Ossipee
Ask Mom & Dad about kayaks, paddleboard, or boat access — lake activities are the main attraction
Louise needs a life jacket on the dock and in the water at all times. Bring one or confirm Mom & Dad have one that fits
Lake Winnipesaukee is cold in early July (~65-70°F). Louise may not last long in the water — have towels and warm clothes ready
Dock edges, boat slip, and rocky shoreline — keep a close eye. Buddy system always

events.text: Morning: Lake time or Castle in the Clouds
Afternoon: Pick up fireworks, supplies for July 4th
Evening: Family dinner, sunset from the dock

items.text: Castle in the Clouds: Historic mountaintop estate in Moultonborough (~20 min drive). Stunning lake views, hiking trails, horseback riding. Louise-friendly grounds. Worth a half-day
Squam Lakes Natural Science Center (Holderness): Live animal exhibits — bears, mountain lions, raptors. Great for kids. ~30 min drive
Weirs Beach: Arcade, mini golf, scenic railway, lake beach. Touristy but fun. ~25 min drive
M/S Mount Washington Cruise: Scenic lake cruise from Weirs Beach. 2.5 hrs. Good for a calm day with the whole family
Fireworks: NH allows consumer fireworks. Phantom Fireworks and local stands along Rte 16 will be stocked for the 4th
Stock up on: sparklers, snacks, drinks, ice, charcoal/propane, s'mores supplies
Check local fireworks shows — Wolfeboro usually does a big display over the lake on July 4th
Coordinate with Kristina's family on dinner plans — cookout at the house?

events.text: 🇺🇸 Happy Fourth of July!
Morning: Lazy lake morning, swimming
Afternoon: Family cookout — burgers, hot dogs, corn
Evening: Sparklers on the dock, watch fireworks over the lake

items.text: Keep it simple — this is the day to just be at the lake with family. No driving needed
Cookout timing: Start grilling around 4-5 PM. Eat by 6. Sunset ~8:25 PM. Fireworks after dark (~9 PM)
Sparklers for the kids at dusk — Kenna can handle her own, supervise Louise closely
Wolfeboro fireworks: Usually launched from the town docks, visible from boats or Cate Park. Check wolfeboro.org for exact time (typically 9:15 PM)
Alton Bay fireworks: Another good show, visible from the water. Usually July 4th evening
From the dock: If you can see across the lake, you may catch multiple town shows from the water. Bring blankets — it drops into the 50s after dark
Private fireworks at the house? NH is a free-fire state — legal without permits. Be considerate of neighbors
Louise + fireworks noise — she may be scared of loud bangs. Have headphones or ear protection ready, or watch from a distance
Bug spray is mandatory for evening dock time. Mosquitoes peak at dusk
If driving to a fireworks show, leave EARLY. Traffic around Wolfeboro and Weirs Beach on July 4th is brutal
Temps drop fast after sunset — 80s during the day, 50s at night. Layers for everyone

events.text: Morning: Boat ride or kayaking
Afternoon: Swimming, dock lounging, nap time for Louise
Dinner out or at the house

items.text: Boat day: If Mom & Dad have a boat, this is the day. Cruise the lake, find a cove, swim off the boat. Louise in a life jacket the whole time
Kayaking/paddleboarding: Calm mornings are best. Kenna would love paddleboarding if available
Libby Museum (Wolfeboro): Quirky little natural history museum. Good for a rainy-day backup
Dinner out: Garwoods in Wolfeboro (lakeside dining), The Cider Press in Center Sandwich, or East of Suez in Center Ossipee for something different
Day 5 of 7. No need to pack the day — the point of this trip is to relax at the lake
If Kristina's family is there, coordinate kid activities. Cousins + lake = entertainment sorted
Downtime for adults while kids play — bring books, enjoy the dock

events.text: Morning: One last swim, soak up the lake
Afternoon: Pack up + last family time
Evening: Family dinner — enjoy the last night
⚠ Pack tonight — early departure tomorrow!

items.text: Pack bags tonight. You need to leave by ~2 PM tomorrow for the drive to BOS
Clean up the house — strip beds, take out trash, leave it nice for Mom & Dad
Double-check nothing left behind: chargers, swimsuits, lake toys, car seats
Return flight is BOS 6:25 PM. Work backwards: 2 hr drive + 30 min car return + 1.5 hr airport buffer = leave Melvin Village by 2:00 PM latest
Morning swim is non-negotiable — last chance until the next visit
Photo ops: Family photo on the dock, kids at the lake, sunset from the deck
If you haven't been to Wolfeboro yet for ice cream, today's the day

events.text: Morning: Last morning at the lake. Final packing
🚗 Drive Melvin Village → BOS (~2 hrs)
Arrive East Boston. Return rental car to Enterprise
Shuttle to terminal. Check in + clear security
✈ WN 754 · BOS → STL · Seats 12A/B/C/D
Arrive STL! Home sweet home 🏠

items.text: Final sweep of the house: chargers, toiletries, swimsuits, kids' items
Car seats into the rental car for the drive back
Say goodbye to Mom & Dad (and Kristina's crew if they're still there)
Leave by 2:00 PM. 2 hr drive + 30 min car return + shuttle + 1.5 hr airport buffer = need to be at gate by 5:45 PM
Enterprise · 15 Transportation Way, East Boston. Due back by 6:00 PM
Fill the tank before returning — there's a gas station near the Enterprise lot
Enterprise shuttle runs continuously to all terminals. ~5 min ride to Terminal B (Southwest)
Enterprise Platinum status — express return. Drop keys and go
Southwest WN 754 · BOS 6:25 PM → STL 8:30 PM
Seats: Meghan 12A, Michael 12B, Louise 12C, Kenna 12D
Confirmation: AXYZFP. Basic fare — carry-on + personal item included
Paid: $921.60 (Visa *4879 $755.10 + Flight Credit $166.50)
I-93 South into Boston can be brutal, especially summer weekday. Check traffic before leaving and allow extra time
Enterprise shuttle to terminal adds 10-15 min. Don't cut it close
Southwest: No assigned seats on Basic fare, but you already have 12A/B/C/D. Check in 24 hrs before (Jul 6 at 6:25 PM CDT) for boarding positions
Louise will be tired and cranky after a week of lake life. Snacks and iPad ready for the flight
Stop for one last lobster roll on the way south if time allows — try the Common Man Roadside in Epsom again
BOS Terminal B (Southwest) has decent food options past security if you're hungry before boarding
2 hr 5 min flight. Home by 8:30 PM. Easy bedtime routine. Trip complete!

budget.savingsNote: 60,686 Venture X miles (~$607 value) for rental car + $166.50 WN flight credit + ~$81 skiplagged savings = ~$854 total savings

checklist.text: Book flights — AA outbound skiplagged (DBDQRA), WN return (AXYZFP)
Book rental car — Enterprise at BOS via Capital One Travel (H-1596322716COUNT)
Coordinate dates with Kristina's family — CONFIRMED same dates (Jul 1–7). They're coming from Nashville.
Confirm with Mom & Dad — house availability, sleeping arrangements for both families
Southwest check-in — Jul 6 at 6:25 PM CDT (24 hrs before return). Both Michael and Meghan check in simultaneously for best boarding positions
Buy car seat / booster for rental car — Louise needs a car seat, Kenna may need a booster. Enterprise doesn't provide them reliably
Stock up on lake supplies — sunscreen, bug spray, swim diapers (if needed), water toys, life jacket for Louise
Plan July 4th cookout — coordinate food with Mom & Dad and Kristina's family. Who's bringing what?
Download offline entertainment for Louise — iPad loaded for 2.5 hr flight + 2 hr drive each way
⚠️ CARRY-ON ONLY — no checked bags! Skiplagged outbound fare (STL→BUF via BOS). Checked bags go to Buffalo. Pack everything in carry-ons + personal items.
Pack swimsuits, lake gear, and warm layers — 80s daytime, 50s at night. Bug spray essential for evening dock time
Pack car seats in carry-on-friendly bag or gate-check — need them for the rental car drive to Melvin Village
Chargers, portable battery pack, camera — lake sunsets are worth capturing
